questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: VE — quality function deployment (QFD):
options: House of Quality. Customer voice → product characteristics. Prioritization matrix
questions.solution: QFD: roof (interrelationships). Targets: benchmark, difficulty, importance. 4 phases: product → parts → process → production.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Systematic Layout Planning (SLP) uses:
questions.solution: SLP: Muther. REL chart (A-E-I-O-U-X). Activity relationships drive layout. Iterative process.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Reorder point (ROP) with safety stock:
options: ROP = d̄ × L̄ + SS (demand during lead time + safety stock)
questions.solution: ROP: when inventory hits this level, order Q. Includes lead time demand and safety buffer.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Toyota Production System: jidoka means:
questions.solution: Jidoka: stop and fix problems at source. Machines detect defects and stop. Workers can stop line. Built-in quality.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: EOQ with quantity discounts:
options: Price break → compare total cost at each discount level
questions.solution: Evaluate TC at each price break EOQ (or adjusted). Minimum TC may be at a price break.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Safety stock formula considers:
options: Demand variability, lead time variability, service level
questions.solution: SS = z × σ_LT. z from service level, σ_LT = standard deviation of demand during lead time.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Dodge-Romig sampling tables:
options: LTPD-based (single, double) and AOQL-based. Minimize inspection. For rectifying inspection
questions.solution: Dodge-Romig: process average p̄ affects plan. Manufacturer's risk considered. AOQL = 1%, 3%, 5% tables.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: CRAFT (Computerized Layout):
options: Heuristic improvement algorithm. Pairwise exchange of departments. Minimize material handling cost
questions.solution: CRAFT: from initial layout. Exchange that reduces cost most. Steepest descent. 2-way and 3-way exchanges.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: FMEA: Risk Priority Number (RPN) = Severity × Occurrence × Detection:
questions.solution: RPN: product of three factors. High RPN = high risk. Used to prioritize corrective actions. Max RPN = 1000.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the CPM network shown (path 1-2-4 = A+B = 5+7, path 1-3-4 = C+D = 2+9), the project duration (critical path) is:
questions.solution: Path durations: 1-2-4 = 12, 1-3-4 = 11. The critical path is the longest = 12 days.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Borg CR-10 scale measures:
questions.solution: Borg CR-10: category-ratio scale 0-10. Used for rating perceived exertion, pain, breathlessness. Subjective assessment.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Seasonal ARIMA (SARIMA):
options: ARIMA(p,d,q)(P,D,Q)_s. s = period (12 for monthly). Seasonal differencing. Example: ARIMA(1,1,1)(1,1,1)₁₂
questions.solution: SARIMA: airline passenger model. ACF spikes at seasonal lags. Extended Box-Jenkins.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The balance (line) efficiency of an assembly line is:
options: Sum of task times ÷ (number of stations × cycle time)
questions.solution: Efficiency = Σti/(N·C); the complement is balance delay (idle fraction).

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Risk management: risk register tracks:
questions.solution: Risk register: document all identified risks. Assess P and I. Plan responses. Monitor. Living document.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: ROP: demand=50/day, lead time=3 days, safety stock=15. ROP=

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Kaizen costing: targets cost reduction during:
questions.solution: Kaizen costing: continuous improvement during manufacturing. Contrast with standard costing. Small incremental reductions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: With α = 0.1, last actual = 200 and last forecast = 180, the next exponential-smoothing forecast is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Returnable transport items (RTI) routing:
options: Pallet/container tracking. Pool management. Empty return vs one-way. Washing/inspection cycle
questions.solution: RTI: Chep, IFCO pools. RFID tracking. Trippage = number of uses. Pool management software.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fixed order quantity (FOQ):
options: Always order same Q. Reorder point system (Q-system). Continuous review
questions.solution: FOQ: min-max system. (s, S) system. Hybrid: order point/order quantity review.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two components of reliability 0.95 and 0.9 are in series. The system reliability is:

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Separation of personnel and equipment:
options: Reduces cost while maintaining quality through redesign
questions.solution: Redesign so personnel and expensive equipment work independently.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) =
questions.solution: OEE = A × P × Q. World-class >85%. Availability = uptime/planned time. Performance = actual/ideal. Quality = good/total.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: QFD Phase 1 (House of Quality) translates:
questions.solution: QFD cascade: Phase 1 (WHATs → HOWs), Phase 2 (HOWs → part chars), Phase 3 (parts → processes), Phase 4 (processes → production).

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Benchmarking involves:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: EOQ: D=2000/yr, S=$30, H=$1/unit/yr. Q*=

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Work sampling: sample size n = Z²·p(1-p)/e². For 95% confidence, e=0.05, p=0.5:

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: MSE penalizes:
options: Large errors more heavily than small errors
questions.solution: MSE = Sum(e_t^2)/n. Squaring penalizes large errors.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Float types:
options: Total float (without delaying project), Free float (without delaying next activity), Independent float
questions.solution: TF = LS - ES = LF - EF. FF = ES_next - EF_current. IF = FF - slack_next.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Agile supply chain responds to:
questions.solution: Agile: responsive to unpredictable demand. Postponement.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Value — use vs esteem vs exchange:
options: Use: functional. Esteem: prestige (luxury). Exchange: resale value. Cost: production cost
questions.solution: Value analysis: identify unnecessary cost. Function: verb + noun. Basic vs secondary functions.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Drum-Buffer-Rope (DBR) drum is:
options: The bottleneck pace that sets production rate
questions.solution: Drum = bottleneck schedule. Buffer protects drum from disruptions. Rope releases material at drum pace.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Line balancing:
options: Assign tasks to stations to minimize idle time. Balance delay = idle time/(CT × n)
questions.solution: Line balancing: precedence diagram, task times. Heuristics: LPT, Kilbridge-Wester, RPW.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fixed cost = ₹50000, price = ₹80/unit, variable cost = ₹50/unit. The break-even quantity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Finite capacity scheduling: dispatching rules:
options: SPT minimizes mean flow time. EDD minimizes maximum lateness. SPT/EDD trade-off
questions.solution: SPT: short jobs first. EDD: due date. CR: critical ratio. Slack: least slack first.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: CDS algorithm (Campbell):
options: Extends Johnson to m machines. k = m-1 subproblems
questions.solution: CDS: create 2-machine problems (machine 1 to k vs machine m-k+1 to m). Pick best makespan.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Line balancing: balance delay = (n×CT - Σt_i)/(n×CT) × 100%. For n=5, CT=10, Σt=45:
questions.solution: Balance delay = idle time / total available time. Efficiency = 1 - balance delay = 90%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Production activity control (PAC):
options: WIP tracking, priority control, input/output control. Bottleneck management
questions.solution: PAC: shop floor. Sequencing rules (SPT, EDD, CR, FIFO). Input/output: control WIP.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Priority sequencing rules:
options: FCFS, SPT, EDD, LPT, CR (critical ratio). SPT minimizes mean flow time. EDD minimizes max lateness
questions.solution: CR = (due date - now)/(remaining processing time). CR < 1 = behind schedule.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: NPV: Initial cost=$10000, annual benefit=$3000, r=10%, n=5 years. NPV≈

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Type I error in SPC:
options: False alarm when process in control
questions.solution: Type I: false alarm. Type II: missed signal.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Time series components:
options: Trend (T), Seasonal (S), Cyclical (C), Random (R). Additive: Y = T+S+C+R. Multiplicative: Y = T×S×C×R
questions.solution: Decomposition: isolate each component. Seasonality: repeating pattern <1 year. Cycle: >1 year.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: RFID in supply chain:
options: Radio-frequency identification. Real-time tracking. Pallet/item level
questions.solution: RFID: tag + reader. Passive (no battery) vs active (battery). Item-level vs pallet-level.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: HML (High-Medium-Low):
options: By unit price. H: high unit cost. M: medium. L: low cost
questions.solution: HML: control expensive items (pilferage, special handling). H: tight control. L: simple.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the CPM network shown (path 1-2-4 = A+B = 3+8, path 1-3-4 = C+D = 2+5), the project duration (critical path) is:
questions.solution: Path durations: 1-2-4 = 11, 1-3-4 = 7. The critical path is the longest = 11 days.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: EOQ: D=8000/yr, S=$40, H=$4/unit/yr. Q*=

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Game theory:
options: Strategic interaction. Zero-sum, two-person. Saddle point, mixed strategies
questions.solution: Saddle point: max(min) = min(max). Mixed: probabilities for each strategy. Value of game.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Flow process chart types: man-type vs material-type:
options: Man-type: worker activities. Material-type: material movements
questions.solution: Man type: worker-focused. Material type: follows material through process. Equipment type: machine usage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the break-even chart shown (fixed cost ₹20000, price ₹80, variable cost ₹60/unit), the marked point where revenue meets total cost occurs at:
questions.solution: BEP = FC/(p − v) = 20000/(80 − 60) = 1000 units, where total revenue = total cost.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Quality cost categories: prevention, appraisal, internal failure, external failure:
options: Prevention + Appraisal = Cost of Conformance. Internal + External = Cost of Non-Conformance
questions.solution: Prevention: training, planning. Appraisal: inspection. Internal: scrap, rework. External: warranty, returns.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Single minute exchange of die (SMED):
options: Goal: < 10 min. Steps: separate internal/external, convert internal→external, streamline
questions.solution: SMED: Shingo. Examples: Toyota stamping 1.5 min. Setup improvement: 50-90% reduction.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Warehouse layout: golden zone:
options: Most frequently picked items placed at waist-to-shoulder height in central aisles
questions.solution: Golden zone: ergonomic picking. Reduces bending and reaching. Slotting optimization.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Tracking signal: TS = Running sum of forecast errors / MAD. Out of control if:
questions.solution: TS: measures bias. RSFE = Σe_t. If TS > 4: underforecasting. If TS < -4: overforecasting.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Supply chain — push-pull boundary:
options: Point where product is linked to customer order. BTO vs BTS. Postponement
questions.solution: Push-pull: Dell BTO (pull), HP printers (push to distribution → pull). Decoupling point.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Statistical Process Control (SPC):
options: Use of control charts to monitor process stability and variability
questions.solution: SPC: common cause (systemic) vs special cause (assignable) variation. Control limits ≠ spec limits.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Supply chain stages:
questions.solution: SCM: material, information, financial flows. Integration across all stages.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Process capability: Cp > 2.0 means:
options: Six sigma capable (less than 2 defects per billion)
questions.solution: Cp = (USL-LSL)/(6*sigma). Cp=2.0: 12 sigma between spec limits. Excellent.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: RFID in supply chain: tag types:
options: Passive (no battery, reader-powered) and active (battery-powered, longer range)
questions.solution: Passive: low cost, short range (<10m). Active: longer range (100m+), higher cost.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A Pareto chart is used in quality control to:
options: Identify the vital few causes of most defects
questions.solution: The Pareto (80/20) chart ranks defect causes so effort targets the most significant few.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Supplier evaluation — weighted criteria:
options: Quality, delivery, cost, flexibility, innovation, sustainability
questions.solution: Supplier: multi-attribute. AHP (Analytic Hierarchy Process). Vendor rating system.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Control limits — 3σ:
options: UCL = CL + 3σ, LCL = CL - 3σ. Type I error: 0.27%. Based on normal assumption
questions.solution: 3σ: economic balance between Type I and Type II. Shewhart (1924). For non-normal: distribution-based limits.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Distribution network design — DC location:
options: Gravity model, p-median, location-allocation. Minimize cost + service level constraint
questions.solution: p-median: find p DCs minimizing weighted distance. Heuristics: add, swap, drop.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cp vs Cpk:
options: Cp: potential capability (centered). Cpk: actual capability (accounts for centering)
questions.solution: Cpk = min(CPU, CPL). CPU = (USL-x̄)/3σ, CPL = (x̄-LSL)/3σ. Cpk ≤ Cp.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Responsive vs efficient supply chain:
options: Responsive: speed, flexibility (fashion). Efficient: low cost, high utilization (commodities)
questions.solution: Fisher matrix: functional vs innovative products. Efficient for functional, responsive for innovative.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PERT activity has to = 2, tm = 8, tp = 12 days. Its expected time is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Customers arrive at 20/hour and spend on average 0.75 hour in the system. The average number in the system is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: SCOR model:
options: Plan → Source → Make → Deliver → Return. 5 management processes. Level 1: 7 metrics
questions.solution: SCOR: Supply Chain Operations Reference. Metric: Perfect order fulfillment, Cash-to-cash.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: String diagram:
options: Thread tracing movement on layout. Distance and frequency of travel
questions.solution: String: actual paths on scale drawing. Thread length = distance traveled. Congestion detection.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The basic fundamental motions identified by the Gilbreths are called:
questions.solution: Therbligs (Gilbreth reversed) are 17–18 elemental hand motions used in micromotion study.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Reliability block diagram: series system. If R1=0.95, R2=0.9, R3=0.85:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A product is used at 20 units/day with a lead time of 5 days. The reorder point (no safety stock) is:
questions.solution: ROP = demand rate × lead time = 20×5 = 100 units.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Network models: minimum spanning tree (MST):
options: Connects all nodes with minimum total edge weight (no cycles)
questions.solution: MST: Prim or Kruskal. n nodes, n-1 edges. Used in facility location, network design.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Using 3σ control limits, the probability of a point falling outside the limits when the process is in control is about:
questions.solution: About 99.73% lie within ±3σ, so ~0.27% fall outside by chance (false alarm).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the CPM network shown (path 1-2-4 = A+B = 5+6, path 1-3-4 = C+D = 2+10), the project duration (critical path) is:
questions.solution: Path durations: 1-2-4 = 11, 1-3-4 = 12. The critical path is the longest = 12 days.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Queuing — M/M/s:
questions.solution: M/M/s: multiple servers. Infinite queue. L_q = P_0·(sρ)^s·ρ/(s!(1-ρ)²). Erlang C.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An activity's normal time/cost are 8 days/₹3000 and crash time/cost are 4 days/₹5000. Its crash cost slope is:
questions.solution: Cost slope = (crash − normal cost)/(normal − crash time) = (5000−3000)/(8−4) = ₹500/day.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A Gantt chart primarily displays:
options: Activity schedules against a time axis
questions.solution: A Gantt (bar) chart shows each task's start/finish along a timeline.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PERT activity has to = 2, tm = 6, tp = 14 days. Its expected time is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PERT activity has to = 3 and tp = 11 days. Its standard deviation is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the Pareto chart of defect causes shown (tallest bar = cause B), the principle being applied says you should first address:
options: Cause B (the tallest bar — the vital few)
questions.solution: The Pareto (80/20) principle prioritizes the 'vital few' tallest bars; cause B contributes the most defects and is tackled first.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Control chart: run of 8 points on same side of centerline indicates:
questions.solution: Western Electric rules: run of 8+ on same side = trend or shift. Investigate.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: SPC — Western Electric rules:
options: Zone rules: 1 point > 3σ, 2 of 3 > 2σ, 4 of 5 > 1σ, 8 on one side → out of control
questions.solution: Western Electric: extended rules. Probability of false alarm (Type I) higher than 3σ alone.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Service level:
options: CSL = probability of no stockout during lead time. Cost trade-off with safety stock
questions.solution: 99% service level: z = 2.33. Higher SL → more safety stock. Diminishing returns.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Work study: method study vs work measurement:
options: Method study: how to do the job (improve method). Work measurement: how long it takes (set time)
questions.solution: Method study: systematic examination. Work measurement: determine standard time. Together: work study.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: 5S methodology: Seiri means:
questions.solution: 5S: Sort (Seiri), Set in order (Seiton), Shine (Seiso), Standardize (Seiketsu), Sustain (Shitsuke). Japanese workplace organization.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: PERT: expected time t_e = (a+4m+b)/6. Variance = ((b-a)/6)^2. For a=5, m=8, b=17:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Predictive maintenance — thermography:
options: Infrared camera. Hot spots: electrical faults, friction, insulation breakdown
questions.solution: Thermography: temperature patterns. ΔT >10°C → investigate. Reflected temperature correction.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Component A (R = 0.95) is in series with a parallel pair B (0.8) and C (0.95). The system reliability is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Performance rating: Westinghouse system rates:
options: Skill, effort, conditions, consistency (each rated A,B,C,D)
questions.solution: Westinghouse: 4 factors. Each A (best) to D (worst). Rating factor = average of 4 ratings.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two components of reliability 0.8 and 0.85 are in parallel. The system reliability is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Reliability — component vs system:
options: Series: R_s = ΠR_i. Parallel: R_p = 1 - Π(1-R_i). k-out-of-n: binomial
questions.solution: k-out-of-n: at least k of n working. Binary reliability. RBD (reliability block diagram).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Consignment inventory:
options: Supplier-owned inventory at buyer location. Payment when consumed. Buyer reduces risk, supplier finances
questions.solution: Consignment: common in retail (books, electronics). VMI often combined. Risks: obsolescence on supplier.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A product is used at 50 units/day with a lead time of 5 days. The reorder point (no safety stock) is:
questions.solution: ROP = demand rate × lead time = 50×5 = 250 units.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Quantity discount model: compare total cost at each price break EOQ:
options: Minimum total cost may not be at the lowest unit price
questions.solution: Evaluate TC at each price break. Optimal may be between breaks if holding cost changes.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Simulation optimization: OptQuest uses:
options: Metaheuristics (tabu search, neural networks, scatter search)
questions.solution: OptQuest: commercial optimization tool. Combines tabu, neural nets, scatter search. Finds near-optimal settings.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Assignment — maximization:
options: Negate costs or use (max - cost) matrix. Hungarian method works
questions.solution: Maximization: convert to minimization by subtracting from maximum.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fixed cost = ₹20000, price = ₹80/unit, variable cost = ₹50/unit. The break-even quantity is:

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: 3PL (Third Party Logistics) provider offers:
questions.solution: 3PL: warehousing, transportation, fulfillment. Allows focus on core competencies. Variable cost vs fixed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Warehousing functions:
options: Receiving, put-away, storage, order picking, packing, shipping
questions.solution: Warehouse: unit load storage. Pallet racks, drive-in, push-back, AS/RS. Slotting optimization.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Cross-docking: goods are:
options: Transferred directly from inbound to outbound (minimal storage)
questions.solution: Cross-dock: receive and ship within 24h. Minimal handling/storage. Requires accurate timing and information.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A process has USL = 112, LSL = 88, mean = 100, σ = 2. Its Cpk is about:
